LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS- DEFENDANT BRIAN MCLAUGHLIN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION EXCEPT THE FIFTH. IT APPEARS UNCONTESTED THAT BRIAN MCLAUGHLIN'S EMPLOYMENT WITH 1750 DIVIS LLC ENDED IN AUGUST 2009. AS SUCH, ALL CLAIMS RELATED TO HIS ACTS OR OMISSIONS DURING THAT PERIOD OF EMPLOYMENT ARE BARRED BY THE RELEVANT STATUTES OF LIMITATION. OVERRULED AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION, FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF AS TO THE OWNERSHIP OF 1750 DIVIS. THE COMPLAINT ITSELF SERVES AS EVIDENCE OF A CONTROVERSY OVER WHO OWNS 1750 DIVIS AND IN WHAT PROPORTION; DECLARATORY RELIEF IN THESE CIRCUMSTANCES IS PROPER. THE COURT DOES NOT CONSIDER THE PURPORTED MOTION TO STRIKE AS THE SPECIAL SETTING IN THIS CASE WAS ONLY SOUGHT FOR THE DEMURRERS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER : MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489 =302/EG

IT APPEARS UNCONTESTED THAT BRIAN MCLAUGHLIN'S EMPLOYMENT WITH 1750 DIVIS LLC ENDED IN AUGUST 2009. is sustained without leave to amend as to all five claims. The face of the cross-complaint shows that all five claims are time-barred. Any obligation by Mr. McLaughlin to put his name on the liquor license accrued more than four years before this lawsuit was filed. Moreover, even if they were not time-barred, all five purported causes of action are not adequately alleged. Mr. Owens has not shown that there is a reasonable possibility that he can allege facts that any of his claims against Mr. McLaughlin is not time-barred or that any of those claims can be adequately supported by the allegation of ultimate facts rather than conclusions of law. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/hk

APPEARANCES - ATTY. STEVEN N. HOLLAND OF RING HUNTER & SCHENONE LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-925-226-8248 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS/CROSS-DEFENDANTS;
ATTY. MARK MALACHOWSKI OF THE MALACHOWSKI & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. 1-415-983-0717 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANT JENNIFER MCLAUGHLIN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to all causes of action except the fifth. It appears uncontested that Brian McLaughlin's employment with 1750 Divis LLC ended in August 2009. As such, all claims related to his acts or omissions during that period of employment are barred by the relevant statutes of limitation. Overruled as to the fifth cause of action, for declaratory relief as to the ownership of 1750 Divis. The Complaint itself serves as evidence of a controversy over who owns 1750 Divis and in what proportion; declaratory relief in these circumstances is proper. The Court does not consider the purported motion to strike as the special setting in this case was only sought for the demurrers.
